_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Presence of symptomatic varicose veins (e.g., aching pain, tiredness, discomfort, ankle/feet edema, eczema, hyperpigmentation, lipodermatosclerosis, venous ulceration, itching, bleeding, superficial thrombophlebitis).
Significant edema, skin changes, or ulceration associated with varicose veins.
Confirmed valvular incompetence of saphenous, perforator, or deep veins by Duplex scan.
Failure of non-pharmacological conservative management (e.g., elastic compression stockings, foot elevation) for significant valvular incompetence or severe symptoms.

clinical_thresholds.value: elastic compression stockings and foot elevation
clinical_thresholds.note: Required for significant edema, skin changes, or ulcer, or incompetence of greater saphenous vein & perforator vein, or greater saphenous vein + perforator vein + deep vein.
To classify location of valvular incompetence.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with details of clinical examination and planned line of treatment

ppd_preauth.q: Was the indication for surgery mentioned?
Was the duplex scan report submitted?
Is there an evidence of nonsurgical/ medical management tried but failed?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Indication for surgery not mentioned in clinical notes.
Duplex scan report not submitted at pre-authorization.
No evidence of nonsurgical/medical management tried and failed prior to intervention.
Clinical photographs not submitted at pre-authorization.
Detailed procedure/operative notes missing at claim submission.
Post-operative clinical photographs missing at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Varicose veins are dilated, tortuous veins caused by dilation of normal veins due to increased venous pressure.
They are visible surface manifestations of an underlying syndrome of venous insufficiency.
Occur in varying severity and in different clinical presentations.
2% of these patients present with skin changes and morbid chronic venous ulcers.
Causes include: Hereditary, history of phlebitis, female, pregnancy, pelvic tumors, prolonged standing.
Varicose veins may also occur secondary to DVT and arteriovenous malformations.

Varicose veins may involve: Greater saphenous vein (GSV), Lesser saphenous vein (LSV) with their tributaries, Perforator veins (which connect them to the deep venous system of the leg), or a combination of the above veins.

Clinical presentation depends on severity and may include: Aching pain in the leg, tiredness, discomfort, ankle/feet edema (especially after prolonged standing), eczema, hyperpigmentation, lipodermatosclerosis, venous ulceration, itching, bleeding, superficial thrombophlebitis.
Patients are categorized according to CEAP (clinical, etiological, anatomical and pathological) status for therapy guidance, documentation, and follow-up.
surgical_options: Saphenofemoral junction ligation and greater saphenous stripping with or without incompetent perforator ligation
Saphenopopliteal junction ligation and lesser saphenous stripping with or without incompetent perforator ligation
Split thickness skin grafting for venous ulcers, if present
additional_information.referral_criteria: Consider deep venous reconstruction in tertiary care hospital if incompetence of greater saphenous vein + perforator vein + deep vein fails surgical treatment.
